rawText:
Latin America in 2026 offers a stark study in contrasts, as Argentina's Javier Milei -- a libertarian firebrand who calls his Peronist rivals "murderers and thieves" and "cavemen" in the very congressional chamber where he outlines his legislative agenda -- celebrates a string of genuine economic successes, while ninety miles from Florida, Cuba's communist regime presides over nationwide blackouts, empty hotels, and a population that has shrunk from 11.2 million to perhaps 8.6 million as citizens flee an economy more broken than at any point since the collapse of the Soviet Union.

Milei's Argentina demonstrates what can happen when radical liberalisation is pursued with ruthless conviction, as the president has passed a major labour reform that makes it cheaper to fire workers and empowers regional salary negotiations, slashed red tape at customs, accumulated 2.7 billion dollars in foreign reserves in the first two months of the year, and dragged monthly inflation down from its catastrophic heights -- achievements that have left him politically stronger than any Argentine president at this stage in their term, despite not commanding a congressional majority.

Yet the Argentine miracle, if it is one, carries an uncomfortable asterisk: the economy is recovering with lower levels of salaried formal employment than before Milei took office, industrial output remains below its December 2023 level despite GDP growth of 4.4 per cent, and polling now shows that unemployment has consistently overtaken inflation as voters' foremost concern -- a shift that threatens to erode the very mandate that made the reforms possible.

Cuba's trajectory runs in precisely the opposite direction, as the Trump administration's capture of Nicolas Maduro severed the island's last reliable energy lifeline, the subsequent fuel blockade has shuttered airlines, idled hospitals, emptied the glitzy coastal hotels built by Gaesa -- the military conglomerate that controls much of the economy -- and reduced a typical monthly salary to the purchasing power of barely a dozen eggs on the black market.

The regime's economic model, which stubbornly misallocates resources toward struggling state industries while sugar exports have fallen by ninety per cent and overall exports in real terms have declined by at least seventy-five per cent since 2000, has been laid bare as perhaps the most comprehensively failed experiment in central planning still operating in the Western hemisphere.

Under intense American pressure, Cuba's rulers have been compelled to make their most significant concessions since Fidel Castro first permitted limited private enterprise in the 1990s -- allowing exiles to own businesses, permitting private firms to import fuel, and releasing political prisoners -- though the Castro family, whose grandson negotiates with Washington while holding no official title, seems likely to retain the real levers of power behind whatever figurehead replaces the widely derided President Diaz-Canel.

The emerging deal between Washington and Havana, modelled on the arrangement imposed on Venezuela, envisions American corporate access to Cuban energy, ports, tourism, and telecoms -- a prospect that Donald Trump, whose organisation registered its trademark in Havana for hotels, casinos, and golf courses as far back as 2008, has coveted for decades and that Cuban-Americans in Florida regard with deep suspicion, fearing the regime will simply pocket concessions and suffocate private business with arbitrary regulations whenever it feels strong enough to do so.

Argentina and Cuba thus represent opposing poles of Latin American reform: one country gambling that the short-term pain of liberalisation -- the factory closures, the shift from well-paid formal jobs to low-paying contract work, the potential exodus from Buenos Aires toward the resource-rich interior -- will eventually yield more competitive businesses and higher wages; the other, a command economy so thoroughly degraded that its central bank may hold no more than three billion dollars in reserves and whose GDP is forecast to contract by 7.2 per cent this year.

What links these seemingly disparate cases is the role of external coercion in shaping domestic policy: Milei's free-trade deal with Europe and his courtship of global capital markets require Argentina to maintain the fiscal discipline and institutional credibility that investors demand, just as Cuba's concessions have been extracted not through internal conviction but through an American blockade so comprehensive that the regime, stripped of Venezuelan oil, Russian fuel shipments, and the foreign currency earned by its exported doctors, faces what one former American official describes as being "over a barrel."

The lesson Latin America offers the world is that neither reform nor ruin follows a straight line -- that Argentina's liberaliser may yet stumble if unemployment outpaces the promised industrial transformation, that Cuba's regime may survive its current crisis through a dirty deal that preserves Castro power behind a democratic facade, and that the distance between a continent's most ambitious reformer and its most stubborn holdout is measured not in ideology alone but in the willingness of leaders to confront, honestly and without paranoid rhetoric, the gap between what their economies promise and what their citizens endure.

overview.title: Reform and Ruin in Latin America

sentences.original: Latin America in 2026 offers a stark study in contrasts, as Argentina's Javier Milei -- a libertarian firebrand who calls his Peronist rivals "murderers and thieves" and "cavemen" in the very congressional chamber where he outlines his legislative agenda -- celebrates a string of genuine economic successes, while ninety miles from Florida, Cuba's communist regime presides over nationwide blackouts, empty hotels, and a population that has shrunk from 11.2 million to perhaps 8.6 million as citizens flee an economy more broken than at any point since the collapse of the Soviet Union.
sentences.translation: 2026년의 라틴아메리카는 극명한 대조의 연구를 보여준다. 아르헨티나의 하비에르 밀레이 -- 입법 안건을 설명하는 바로 그 의회 회의장에서 페론주의 경쟁자들을 "살인자와 도둑" 그리고 "혈거인"이라 부르는 자유지상주의 선동가 -- 가 일련의 진정한 경제적 성과를 축하하는 동안, 플로리다에서 90마일 떨어진 곳에서 쿠바의 공산 정권은 전국적인 정전, 텅 빈 호텔, 소련 붕괴 이후 어느 때보다 더 망가진 경제를 탈출하면서 1120만에서 아마 860만으로 줄어든 인구를 관장하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Latin America in 2026 offers a stark study in contrasts
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사 + 목적어)

grammarElements.text: as Argentina's Javier Milei celebrates ...
grammarElements.label: as절 (동시 상황)

grammarElements.text: while Cuba's communist regime presides over ...
grammarElements.label: while절 (대조)

grammarElements.text: a libertarian firebrand who calls his Peronist rivals ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The party's firebrand leader alienated moderates but energised the base.
The outgoing minister presided over a period of economic decline.

expressions.usage: 두 대상이 극단적으로 다른 양상을 보일 때 서두에서 사용하는 분석적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Region] offers a stark study in contrasts, as [success story], while [failure story]
patterns.explanation: 같은 지역 내 성공과 실패를 대조적으로 병치하며 분석의 틀을 세우는 패턴
examples: Asia offers a stark study in contrasts, as South Korea's tech sector booms, while Japan's electronics industry continues to retreat.
The Eurozone offers a stark study in contrasts, as Ireland's growth surges, while Italy's debt burden deepens.

drillQuestions.question: Latin America in 2026 offers a stark study in ______.

sentences.notes: as절과 while절의 대조가 아르헨티나의 개혁 성과와 쿠바의 붕괴를 한 문장 안에서 극적으로 병치한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a stark study in contrasts
a libertarian firebrand
celebrates a string of genuine economic successes
presides over nationwide blackouts

sentences.original: Milei's Argentina demonstrates what can happen when radical liberalisation is pursued with ruthless conviction, as the president has passed a major labour reform that makes it cheaper to fire workers and empowers regional salary negotiations, slashed red tape at customs, accumulated 2.7 billion dollars in foreign reserves in the first two months of the year, and dragged monthly inflation down from its catastrophic heights -- achievements that have left him politically stronger than any Argentine president at this stage in their term, despite not commanding a congressional majority.
sentences.translation: 밀레이의 아르헨티나는 급진적 자유화가 무자비한 확신으로 추진될 때 어떤 일이 일어날 수 있는지를 보여준다. 대통령은 해고 비용을 낮추고 지역별 임금 협상을 가능하게 하는 주요 노동 개혁을 통과시켰고, 세관의 규제를 대폭 줄였으며, 올해 첫 두 달 동안 27억 달러의 외환보유고를 축적했고, 월간 인플레이션을 재앙적 수준에서 끌어내렸다 -- 의회 과반을 장악하지 못함에도 불구하고 임기 이 시점에서 어떤 아르헨티나 대통령보다 정치적으로 강한 위치에 놓이게 한 성과들이다.

grammarElements.text: Milei's Argentina demonstrates
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사)

grammarElements.text: what can happen when radical liberalisation is pursued with ruthless conviction
grammarElements.label: what절 + when절

grammarElements.text: has passed ..., slashed ..., accumulated ..., and dragged ...
grammarElements.label: 사중 병렬 동사

grammarElements.text: achievements that have left him politically stronger
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The CEO was ruthless in cutting costs that others considered untouchable.

expressions.usage: 관료주의적 절차나 불필요한 규제를 크게 축소할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] demonstrates what can happen when [approach] is pursued with [intensity]
patterns.explanation: 특정 사례가 급진적 접근의 결과를 보여준다고 소개하는 패턴
examples: Singapore demonstrates what can happen when meritocracy is pursued with relentless consistency.
The startup demonstrates what can happen when innovation is pursued with obsessive focus.

drillQuestions.question: Milei's Argentina demonstrates what can happen when radical liberalisation is pursued with ______ conviction.

sentences.notes: 사중 병렬 동사(passed, slashed, accumulated, dragged)가 개혁의 속도와 범위를 열거하며 독자에게 압도적 인상을 준다.

koreanAlignment.en: radical liberalisation is pursued with ruthless conviction
koreanAlignment.ko: 급진적 자유화가 무자비한 확신으로 추진될 때

koreanAlignment.en: slashed red tape at customs
dragged monthly inflation down from its catastrophic heights
despite not commanding a congressional majority

sentences.original: Yet the Argentine miracle, if it is one, carries an uncomfortable asterisk: the economy is recovering with lower levels of salaried formal employment than before Milei took office, industrial output remains below its December 2023 level despite GDP growth of 4.4 per cent, and polling now shows that unemployment has consistently overtaken inflation as voters' foremost concern -- a shift that threatens to erode the very mandate that made the reforms possible.
sentences.translation: 그러나 아르헨티나의 기적은 -- 기적이라 할 수 있다면 -- 불편한 단서를 달고 있다: 밀레이 취임 전보다 낮은 수준의 정규직 고용과 함께 경제가 회복되고 있으며, 산업 생산량은 4.4퍼센트의 GDP 성장에도 불구하고 2023년 12월 수준 이하에 머물러 있고, 여론조사에서 실업이 인플레이션을 제치고 유권자의 최대 관심사로 올라섰다 -- 개혁을 가능하게 한 바로 그 민의를 잠식할 위험이 있는 변화이다.

grammarElements.text: Yet the Argentine miracle, if it is one
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 삽입 조건절

grammarElements.text: carries an uncomfortable asterisk
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어 (은유적)

grammarElements.text: the economy is recovering ..., industrial output remains ..., and polling shows ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 삼중 병렬 설명

grammarElements.text: a shift that threatens to erode the very mandate
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The team's record carries an asterisk because of the shortened season.

expressions.usage: 성과의 기반이 되는 정치적 지지가 서서히 무너지는 상황을 묘사

patterns.pattern: Yet [achievement], if it is one, carries an uncomfortable asterisk: [caveat 1], [caveat 2], and [caveat 3]
patterns.explanation: 성과에 의문을 제기하며 그 이면의 부정적 조건을 삼중으로 나열하는 패턴
examples: Yet the recovery, if it is one, carries an uncomfortable asterisk: wages have not kept pace, consumer confidence remains fragile, and the trade deficit has widened.
Yet the ceasefire, if it holds, carries an uncomfortable asterisk: displacement continues, aid routes remain blocked, and neither side has disarmed.

drillQuestions.question: The Argentine miracle carries an uncomfortable ______.

sentences.notes: if it is one이라는 삽입이 기적 자체에 의문을 투사하며, asterisk라는 은유가 성과의 조건부적 성격을 효과적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the Argentine miracle, if it is one
koreanAlignment.ko: 아르헨티나의 기적은, 기적이라 할 수 있다면

koreanAlignment.en: carries an uncomfortable asterisk
unemployment has consistently overtaken inflation as voters' foremost concern
koreanAlignment.ko: 실업이 인플레이션을 제치고 유권자의 최대 관심사로 올라섰다

koreanAlignment.en: erode the very mandate that made the reforms possible
koreanAlignment.ko: 개혁을 가능하게 한 바로 그 민의를 잠식하다

pronunciationNotes: asterisk: /ˈæs.tə.rɪsk/ 첫 음절에 강세, 마지막 /sk/ 주의

sentences.original: Cuba's trajectory runs in precisely the opposite direction, as the Trump administration's capture of Nicolas Maduro severed the island's last reliable energy lifeline, the subsequent fuel blockade has shuttered airlines, idled hospitals, emptied the glitzy coastal hotels built by Gaesa -- the military conglomerate that controls much of the economy -- and reduced a typical monthly salary to the purchasing power of barely a dozen eggs on the black market.
sentences.translation: 쿠바의 궤적은 정확히 반대 방향으로 나아가고 있다. 트럼프 행정부의 니콜라스 마두로 체포가 섬의 마지막 신뢰할 수 있는 에너지 생명줄을 끊어버렸고, 이후의 연료 봉쇄는 항공사를 멈추게 하고, 병원을 공전시키고, 가에사 -- 경제의 상당 부분을 통제하는 군사 대기업 -- 가 건설한 화려한 해안가 호텔을 비우고, 평균 월급의 구매력을 암시장에서 겨우 달걀 12개 수준으로 떨어뜨렸다.

grammarElements.text: Cuba's trajectory runs in precisely the opposite direction
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사 + 부사구)

grammarElements.text: as the Trump administration's capture of Nicolas Maduro severed ...
grammarElements.label: as절 (원인)

grammarElements.text: has shuttered ..., idled ..., emptied ..., and reduced ...
grammarElements.label: 사중 병렬 동사

grammarElements.text: the military conglomerate that controls much of the economy
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명 (삽입)

vocabulary.example: The military conglomerate owns everything from hotels to cement factories.

expressions.usage: 핵심적인 지원이나 연결이 단절되었을 때 사용하는 강한 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject]'s trajectory runs in precisely the opposite direction, as [cause] severed [vital connection]
patterns.explanation: 앞의 사례와 정반대 방향으로 가는 사례를 도입하며 원인을 제시하는 전환 패턴
examples: Japan's trajectory runs in precisely the opposite direction, as demographic decline severed the link between growth and prosperity.
The rival's trajectory runs in precisely the opposite direction, as mismanagement severed its connection to its customer base.

drillQuestions.question: Cuba's trajectory runs in precisely the ______ direction.

sentences.notes: 사중 병렬 동사(shuttered, idled, emptied, reduced)가 쿠바 경제 붕괴의 단계적 파급을 한 문장으로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: runs in precisely the opposite direction
severed the island's last reliable energy lifeline
koreanAlignment.ko: 섬의 마지막 신뢰할 수 있는 에너지 생명줄을 끊어버렸다

koreanAlignment.en: the military conglomerate that controls much of the economy
koreanAlignment.ko: 경제의 상당 부분을 통제하는 군사 대기업

koreanAlignment.en: barely a dozen eggs on the black market

sentences.original: The regime's economic model, which stubbornly misallocates resources toward struggling state industries while sugar exports have fallen by ninety per cent and overall exports in real terms have declined by at least seventy-five per cent since 2000, has been laid bare as perhaps the most comprehensively failed experiment in central planning still operating in the Western hemisphere.
sentences.translation: 설탕 수출이 90퍼센트 감소하고 전체 수출이 실질 기준으로 2000년 이후 최소 75퍼센트 하락한 와중에도 고전하는 국영 산업에 완고하게 자원을 잘못 배분해 온 이 정권의 경제 모델은, 서반구에서 아직 작동하고 있는 중앙계획의 가장 포괄적으로 실패한 실험으로 적나라하게 드러났다.

grammarElements.text: The regime's economic model, which stubbornly misallocates resources ...
grammarElements.label: 관계절이 삽입된 주어

grammarElements.text: has been laid bare
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 수동태

grammarElements.text: as perhaps the most comprehensively failed experiment in central planning
grammarElements.label: as + 보어

grammarElements.text: while sugar exports have fallen ... and overall exports have declined ...
grammarElements.label: while 대조절

vocabulary.example: Governments that misallocate resources often face crises when external conditions change.

expressions.usage: 오래 감춰진 실체나 실패가 더 이상 숨길 수 없을 정도로 노출되었을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Model/system] has been laid bare as [superlative judgment]
patterns.explanation: 특정 모델이나 시스템이 궁극적 판정을 받았음을 선언하는 패턴
examples: The outsourcing model has been laid bare as perhaps the costliest shortcut in the industry's history.
The funding structure has been laid bare as perhaps the most fragile arrangement ever approved by regulators.

drillQuestions.question: The regime's economic model has been laid ______ as the most comprehensively failed experiment.

sentences.notes: most comprehensively failed라는 최상급 표현이 쿠바 중앙계획 경제에 대한 최종 판결의 무게를 실어준다.

koreanAlignment.en: stubbornly misallocates resources toward struggling state industries
koreanAlignment.ko: 고전하는 국영 산업에 완고하게 자원을 잘못 배분하는

koreanAlignment.en: sugar exports have fallen by ninety per cent
has been laid bare as
the most comprehensively failed experiment in central planning

sentences.original: Under intense American pressure, Cuba's rulers have been compelled to make their most significant concessions since Fidel Castro first permitted limited private enterprise in the 1990s -- allowing exiles to own businesses, permitting private firms to import fuel, and releasing political prisoners -- though the Castro family, whose grandson negotiates with Washington while holding no official title, seems likely to retain the real levers of power behind whatever figurehead replaces the widely derided President Diaz-Canel.
sentences.translation: 강력한 미국의 압박 아래, 쿠바 지도부는 피델 카스트로가 1990년대에 제한적 민간 기업을 처음 허용한 이래 가장 중대한 양보를 하도록 강제되었다 -- 망명자의 사업 소유 허용, 민간 기업의 연료 수입 허용, 정치범 석방 -- 그러나 공식 직함 없이 워싱턴과 협상하는 손자를 둔 카스트로 가문은, 널리 조롱받는 디아스카넬 대통령을 대체하는 허수아비가 누구이든 그 뒤에서 실질적인 권력의 지렛대를 유지할 가능성이 높다.

grammarElements.text: Under intense American pressure
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 부사어

grammarElements.text: Cuba's rulers have been compelled to make ...
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 수동태 + to부정사

grammarElements.text: allowing ..., permitting ..., and releasing ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 분사구문 (양보의 내용)

grammarElements.text: though the Castro family seems likely to retain the real levers of power
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (though)

vocabulary.example: The chairman was widely seen as a figurehead with no real influence on decisions.
The union won several concessions from management during the negotiations.

expressions.phrase: retain the real levers of power
expressions.usage: 형식적으로는 권력을 넘긴 것처럼 보이지만 실권을 쥐고 있을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Under [pressure], [subject] has been compelled to [concession] -- though [qualifier about real power]
patterns.explanation: 외부 압력에 의한 양보를 인정하면서도 실질적 권력 유지를 지적하는 패턴
examples: Under shareholder pressure, the board has been compelled to replace the CEO -- though the outgoing leader's allies still dominate key committees.
Under regulatory pressure, the bank has been compelled to divest the unit -- though its influence over the buyer remains considerable.

drillQuestions.question: Cuba's rulers have been compelled to make their most significant ______ since the 1990s.

sentences.notes: though절이 양보의 진정성에 즉각 의문을 제기하며, figurehead라는 단어가 형식적 변화 뒤의 실질적 연속성을 꿰뚫는다.

koreanAlignment.en: Under intense American pressure
their most significant concessions
retain the real levers of power
whatever figurehead replaces

sentences.original: The emerging deal between Washington and Havana, modelled on the arrangement imposed on Venezuela, envisions American corporate access to Cuban energy, ports, tourism, and telecoms -- a prospect that Donald Trump, whose organisation registered its trademark in Havana for hotels, casinos, and golf courses as far back as 2008, has coveted for decades and that Cuban-Americans in Florida regard with deep suspicion, fearing the regime will simply pocket concessions and suffocate private business with arbitrary regulations whenever it feels strong enough to do so.
sentences.translation: 베네수엘라에 부과된 합의를 모델로 한 워싱턴과 아바나 간의 부상하는 합의는 쿠바의 에너지, 항만, 관광, 통신에 대한 미국 기업의 접근을 구상하고 있다 -- 2008년에 이미 아바나에서 호텔, 카지노, 골프장에 대한 상표를 등록한 트럼프가 수십 년간 탐내온 전망이자, 플로리다의 쿠바계 미국인들이 정권이 양보를 챙기고 충분히 강하다고 느낄 때마다 자의적 규제로 민간 사업을 질식시킬 것이라고 우려하며 깊은 의구심으로 바라보는 전망이다.

grammarElements.text: The emerging deal, modelled on the arrangement imposed on Venezuela
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 과거분사 수식

grammarElements.text: envisions
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: American corporate access to Cuban energy, ports, tourism, and telecoms
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: a prospect that Trump has coveted ... and that Cuban-Americans regard ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 이중 관계절

vocabulary.example: Foreign investors have long coveted access to the island's untapped market.

expressions.phrase: suffocate private business with arbitrary regulations
expressions.usage: 정부가 규제를 무기로 사용해 민간 활동을 억압할 때 사용하는 비판적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Deal] envisions [access] -- a prospect that [party A] has coveted and that [party B] regards with suspicion
patterns.explanation: 하나의 전망을 두 상반된 이해관계자의 시각으로 동시에 조명하는 패턴
examples: The merger envisions shared platforms -- a prospect that investors have celebrated and that regulators regard with concern.
The reform envisions open markets -- a prospect that exporters have welcomed and that domestic producers regard with alarm.

drillQuestions.question: The emerging deal envisions American corporate ______ to Cuban energy, ports, tourism, and telecoms.

sentences.notes: 트럼프의 2008년 상표 등록이라는 사실이 정치와 사업의 교차를 한 줄로 폭로하며, 이중 관계절이 같은 합의에 대한 상반된 시선을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: modelled on the arrangement imposed on Venezuela
has coveted for decades
regard with deep suspicion
suffocate private business with arbitrary regulations

sentences.original: Argentina and Cuba thus represent opposing poles of Latin American reform: one country gambling that the short-term pain of liberalisation -- the factory closures, the shift from well-paid formal jobs to low-paying contract work, the potential exodus from Buenos Aires toward the resource-rich interior -- will eventually yield more competitive businesses and higher wages; the other, a command economy so thoroughly degraded that its central bank may hold no more than three billion dollars in reserves and whose GDP is forecast to contract by 7.2 per cent this year.
sentences.translation: 따라서 아르헨티나와 쿠바는 라틴아메리카 개혁의 양극단을 대표한다: 한쪽은 자유화의 단기적 고통 -- 공장 폐쇄, 고임금 정규직에서 저임금 계약직으로의 전환, 부에노스아이레스에서 자원이 풍부한 내륙으로의 잠재적 이탈 -- 이 궁극적으로 더 경쟁력 있는 기업과 더 높은 임금을 가져다줄 것이라는 도박을 하는 나라이고, 다른 쪽은 중앙은행이 30억 달러 이하의 외환보유고를 보유하고 올해 GDP가 7.2퍼센트 축소될 것으로 전망되는 철저히 퇴락한 계획 경제이다.

grammarElements.text: Argentina and Cuba thus represent opposing poles
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사 + 목적어)

grammarElements.text: one country gambling that ... will eventually yield ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 첫 번째 대조항

grammarElements.text: the other, a command economy so thoroughly degraded that ...
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 두 번째 대조항

grammarElements.text: the factory closures, the shift ..., the potential exodus ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 (삼중 나열)

vocabulary.example: Years of neglect left the infrastructure in a thoroughly degraded state.

expressions.usage: 같은 범주 내에서 완전히 반대되는 두 사례를 비교할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [A] and [B] represent opposing poles of [domain]: one [approach]; the other, [contrasting state]
patterns.explanation: 두 사례를 같은 범주의 양극단으로 배치하여 대조하는 종합 패턴
examples: Norway and Greece represent opposing poles of fiscal management: one saving methodically; the other, borrowing recklessly.
Tesla and General Motors represent opposing poles of automotive strategy: one betting everything on electric; the other, hedging across technologies.

drillQuestions.question: Argentina and Cuba represent opposing ______ of Latin American reform.

sentences.notes: 콜론과 세미콜론을 사용해 두 나라를 구조적으로 병치하며, 대시 삽입이 자유화의 구체적 고통을 열거한다.

koreanAlignment.en: opposing poles of Latin American reform
the short-term pain of liberalisation
a command economy so thoroughly degraded
whose GDP is forecast to contract by 7.2 per cent

sentences.original: What links these seemingly disparate cases is the role of external coercion in shaping domestic policy: Milei's free-trade deal with Europe and his courtship of global capital markets require Argentina to maintain the fiscal discipline and institutional credibility that investors demand, just as Cuba's concessions have been extracted not through internal conviction but through an American blockade so comprehensive that the regime, stripped of Venezuelan oil, Russian fuel shipments, and the foreign currency earned by its exported doctors, faces what one former American official describes as being "over a barrel."
sentences.translation: 겉보기에 이질적인 이 두 사례를 연결하는 것은 국내 정책 형성에서 외부 강제의 역할이다: 밀레이의 유럽과의 자유무역 협정과 글로벌 자본시장에 대한 구애는 아르헨티나가 투자자들이 요구하는 재정 규율과 제도적 신뢰성을 유지하도록 요구하며, 쿠바의 양보 역시 내부 확신이 아니라 베네수엘라 석유, 러시아 연료 수송, 파견 의사들이 벌어들인 외화를 박탈당한 정권이 한 전직 미국 관리의 표현대로 "꼼짝 못 하는" 상태에 처하게 한 포괄적 미국 봉쇄를 통해 추출된 것이다.

grammarElements.text: What links these seemingly disparate cases
grammarElements.label: What 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: is the role of external coercion in shaping domestic policy
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: just as Cuba's concessions have been extracted not through ... but through ...
grammarElements.label: just as 비교절 + not A but B

grammarElements.text: stripped of Venezuelan oil, Russian fuel shipments, and the foreign currency ...
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식 (삼중 나열)

vocabulary.example: The reforms were achieved through external coercion rather than domestic consensus.

expressions.usage: 협상에서 상대에게 선택지가 없어 어쩔 수 없이 따를 수밖에 없는 상황을 표현하는 관용구

patterns.pattern: What links these [seemingly different cases] is the role of [common factor]: [case A] requires [discipline], just as [case B]'s [outcome] has been extracted through [force]
patterns.explanation: 이질적 사례들의 공통 동인을 밝히고 just as로 병렬 비교하는 종합 패턴
examples: What links these seemingly different bankruptcies is the role of cheap debt: the tech firm required constant refinancing, just as the retailer's expansion had been fuelled by loans it could never repay.
What links these seemingly different protests is the role of social media: the students organised through encrypted chat, just as the workers' grievances had been amplified through viral posts.

drillQuestions.question: What links these seemingly disparate cases is the role of external ______.

sentences.notes: over a barrel이라는 관용구가 쿠바의 궁지를 생생하게 전달하며, just as가 개혁국과 고수국 모두 외부 힘에 의해 움직인다는 핵심 통찰을 연결한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What links these seemingly disparate cases
koreanAlignment.ko: 겉보기에 이질적인 이 두 사례를 연결하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: external coercion in shaping domestic policy
koreanAlignment.ko: 국내 정책 형성에서 외부 강제의 역할

koreanAlignment.en: extracted not through internal conviction but through an American blockade
koreanAlignment.ko: 내부 확신이 아니라 미국 봉쇄를 통해 추출된

koreanAlignment.en: over a barrel

sentences.original: The lesson Latin America offers the world is that neither reform nor ruin follows a straight line -- that Argentina's liberaliser may yet stumble if unemployment outpaces the promised industrial transformation, that Cuba's regime may survive its current crisis through a dirty deal that preserves Castro power behind a democratic facade, and that the distance between a continent's most ambitious reformer and its most stubborn holdout is measured not in ideology alone but in the willingness of leaders to confront, honestly and without paranoid rhetoric, the gap between what their economies promise and what their citizens endure.
sentences.translation: 라틴아메리카가 세계에 제공하는 교훈은 개혁도 파멸도 직선을 따르지 않는다는 것이다 -- 아르헨티나의 자유화론자는 실업이 약속된 산업 전환을 앞지르면 아직 비틀거릴 수 있고, 쿠바 정권은 민주적 외관 뒤에 카스트로 권력을 보존하는 불결한 거래를 통해 현재 위기에서 살아남을 수 있으며, 대륙의 가장 야심 찬 개혁가와 가장 완고한 버티기 세력 사이의 거리는 이념만으로 측정되는 것이 아니라, 자국 경제가 약속하는 것과 시민이 감내하는 것 사이의 간극을 편집증적 수사 없이 정직하게 직면하려는 지도자의 의지로 측정된다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The lesson Latin America offers the world
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 생략형)

grammarElements.text: is that neither reform nor ruin follows a straight line
grammarElements.label: be동사 + that절 보어

grammarElements.text: that Argentina's liberaliser may yet stumble ..., that Cuba's regime may survive ..., and that the distance ... is measured ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 that절 (구체화)

grammarElements.text: not in ideology alone but in the willingness of leaders to confront ...
grammarElements.label: not A but B 구문

vocabulary.example: The democratic facade crumbled as soon as the opposition won seats.
Cuba remains the last holdout against market reform in the region.

expressions.phrase: neither reform nor ruin follows a straight line
expressions.usage: 역사적 과정의 비선형성과 예측 불가능성을 강조하는 결론적 표현

patterns.pattern: The lesson [subject] offers is that neither [A] nor [B] follows a straight line -- that [possibility 1], that [possibility 2], and that [deeper truth]
patterns.explanation: 복수의 가능성을 열어두며 직선적 서사를 거부하는 결론 패턴
examples: The lesson history offers is that neither progress nor decline follows a straight line -- that democracies can backslide, that autocracies can reform, and that the outcome depends on choices not yet made.
The lesson the market offers is that neither bull nor bear follows a straight line -- that rallies can stall, that crashes can reverse, and that timing matters more than conviction.

drillQuestions.question: The lesson Latin America offers is that neither reform nor ruin follows a straight ______.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문장이 개혁과 파멸 모두의 비선형성을 선언하면서, the gap between what economies promise and what citizens endure라는 구절이 글 전체를 관통하는 핵심 질문으로 마무리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: neither reform nor ruin follows a straight line
a dirty deal that preserves Castro power behind a democratic facade
koreanAlignment.ko: 민주적 외관 뒤에 카스트로 권력을 보존하는 불결한 거래

koreanAlignment.en: the most ambitious reformer and its most stubborn holdout
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 야심 찬 개혁가와 가장 완고한 버티기 세력

koreanAlignment.en: the gap between what their economies promise and what their citizens endure
koreanAlignment.ko: 경제가 약속하는 것과 시민이 감내하는 것 사이의 간극

pronunciationNotes: facade: /fəˈsɑːd/ 두 번째 음절에 강세, 프랑스어 차용
